Open Source · Python 3.10+

Reproducible Network Topologies
for CORE Emulation

Generate complex, deterministic CORE network topologies from declarative scenario XML files — via a rich Web GUI or a single CLI command.

What it does

Everything you need to model complex networks

CORE TopoGen handles topology planning, service wiring, vulnerability injection, and artifact generation — all from a single scenario file.

🔁

Deterministic Previews

Pin an RNG seed to lock in host expansion, router placement, connectivity, services, segmentation, and vulnerability assignment across every run.

🖥️

Web GUI Editor

Edit scenarios in the browser with a live preview, Full Preview modal, seed badge, and interactive topology graph — no XML knowledge required.

⌨️

CLI Interface

Run headless with python -m core_topo_gen.cli --xml scenario.xml --seed 42 for scripted pipelines and CI workflows.

🤖

AI Generator (MCP)

Use Ollama + a local MCP server to mutate scenario drafts with natural language. The model calls narrow tools; the backend does the actual planning and validation.

📋

Live Log Dock

Stream run output in real time. Filter by log level or text/regex, and toggle auto-follow for long executions — all from the browser.

🔌

Hardware-in-the-Loop

Manage HITL attachments in the editor, apply Proxmox bridge rewiring from the browser, and keep topologies deterministic by constraining interface attachments.

🔐

Segmentation & Vulnerabilities

Generate NAT/DNAT segmentation rules and docker-compose vulnerability attachments per node, with network_mode: none enforced per service.

📊

Reports & Artifacts

Every run emits Markdown reports, JSON summaries, per-run connectivity CSVs, traffic scripts, and segmentation rules to predictable output directories.

🐳

Docker Compose Deploy

Run the full HTTPS Web UI with docker compose up -d --build. nginx serves 80/443, attack-graph PDF export includes Graphviz out of the box.

Technical Report

CORE Network Emulator

Ahrenholz et al. · The Common Open Research Emulator (CORE) provides real-time network emulation on which CORE TopoGen automates topology generation.

Site →

Get started

Up and running in minutes

Requires Python 3.10+ and uv. Optionally install Graphviz for attack-graph PDF export.

# Install dependencies uv sync --extra dev # Start the Web UI uv run python webapp/app_backend.py # Or use named mode scripts bash scripts/run_webui_mode.sh bash scripts/run_webui_local.sh --web-port 9090 bash scripts/run_webui_remote.sh --core-host 10.0.0.50 --core-port 50051
# Create and activate virtual environment python -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate # Install requirements pip install --upgrade pip pip install -r requirements.txt # Run the Web UI python webapp/app_backend.py
# Build and start the HTTPS stack docker compose up -d --build # Open the UI and verify health open https://localhost curl -k https://localhost/healthz # Stop the stack docker compose down
# Run headless with a seed for reproducibility uv run python -m core_topo_gen.cli --xml path/to/scenario.xml --seed 42 --verbose # Or with pip/venv python -m core_topo_gen.cli --xml scenario.xml --seed 42

Open source

Ready to build your first topology?

CORE TopoGen is open source. Pull requests, issue reports, and documentation improvements are all welcome.
